posts.text: Coming from a chief executive, Franklin Roosevelt’s anti-business rhetoric would shock us today, argues one historian. “With a glee worthy of Donald Trump or Boris Johnson, Roosevelt from the start had luxuriated in shock theatre” https://www.economist.com/by-invitation/2026/04/08/franklin-roosevelt-brilliant-commander-in-chief-terrible-chief-executive?taid=69e500b24d228600010465fc&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter
posts.author: TheEconomist

posts.text:
The plot thickens:

On Friday, at 8:24 AM ET, there was a sudden surge in buying volume in S&P 500 futures as $325 million worth of longs were purchased.

This is the same exact timestamp that $760 million worth of oil shorts were taken.

Just 21 minutes later, Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi said the "Strait of Hormuz is declared completely open."

By 12:45 PM ET, S&P 500 futures had surged to a new record high with this $325 million trade profiting +$50 million in 4 hours.

That's +$50 million over the same exact timeline as oil traders made +$70 million on shorts.

What is happening here?
posts.author: KobeissiLetter

posts.text: Starting to think that Jimmy Carter did a better job at handling Iran…🤣🤣
